title: card-skill article-diagram mode input
description: Structured input for in-article compression formula cards. The default shape is a compression pack: formula, one sentence, and a supporting structure map. Legacy concept-map, process-flow, and boundary-model inputs are still accepted for compatibility. Visible text should follow the source article or requested language unless the user explicitly asks for translation or bilingual output.

family.description: Diagram family. Pick the layout before writing labels.

aspect.description: Canvas ratio for article body placement. Compression formula cards default to body-2-1 and expand to body-3-2 when dense; legacy diagrams default to body-3-2.

design.description: Optional Quiet Paper design system name. Defaults to stripe.

title.description: Short diagram title. It should name the relationship, not the output format. Use the source or requested language for visible text.

subtitle.description: Optional short context line. Use the source or requested language for visible text.

formula.description: Compression-pack formula. Express the core relation, invariant, or transformation in compact visible language.

sentence.description: Compression-pack one-sentence takeaway. This is the human-readable theorem.

render_plan.description: Compression-pack render plan. auto defaults to a formula card containing formula and sentence only. Use structure or split only when explicitly producing the supporting structure view.

structure.description: Compression-pack structure map that supports the formula and sentence.

label.description: Visible structure node label. Use the source or requested language.

note.description: Optional visible node note. Keep it shorter than the label.

label.description: Optional visible relationship label. Use it only when it changes interpretation.

nodes.description: Legacy family visible concepts, steps, or entities. Keep labels short and in the source or requested language.

label.description: Visible node label. Use the source or requested language.

note.description: Optional visible node note. Use the source or requested language.

zone.description: Required for boundary-model nodes; must match a zone id.

links.description: Optional relationships between nodes. Links must point to known node ids.

label.description: Optional visible relationship label. Use the source or requested language.

zones.description: Required for boundary-model. Zones render as fixed nested regions.

label.description: Visible zone label. Use the source or requested language.

description.description: Optional visible zone description. Use the source or requested language.

caption.description: Optional one-sentence interpretation. Keep it short and in the source or requested language.

source.description: Optional source line in the colophon.

logo.description: Optional path to a brand logo or avatar image. Omit for an unbranded output.

brand_name.description: Optional brand or author name in the colophon footer. Omit for an unbranded output.

examples.title: Safety lives at the boundary
examples.formula: Safety = single writer + shared trace + bounded tools
examples.sentence: Multi-agent work stays safe when only one execution line writes while helpers contribute judgment through a shared trace.
examples.caption: The structure map explains why parallel intelligence still needs a single write boundary.
